title: Second-order n8n expression injection via Form node HTML field
description: The Form node's prepareFormFields() helper iterates over the resolvable expressions embedded in an HTML form field and calls context.evaluateExpression() on each before rendering the form. When an HTML field interpolates a value originally supplied by an unauthenticated user (e.g. a previously submitted form value) and that resolved value begins with an '=' character, n8n treats the resolved string as a new expression and evaluates it again. This double evaluation lets an unauthenticated attacker inject and execute arbitrary n8n expressions within the expression context, which, when chained with a sandbox escape, can lead to remote code execution on the n8n host.
